_meta.notes: Second town east, between Mudmoth and Saltgale. Sits in a brackish wetland — the 'fen' — where tallow-reeds grow chest-high. The reeds yield a waxy fiber rendered into 'tallow-wax', a clean-burning candle base used in the elven counting houses (the slate boards need clear non-smoking light). Salt-folk wholesalers come here to negotiate the wax season.

tagline: Reeds taller than you. The wax burns clean enough to write by.
ambientPool: Reeds rustle in unbroken sound for hundreds of yards in every direction.
A reed-cutter wades to thigh depth and works one row at a time.
Wax is rendered in open-air kettles; the steam smells faintly herbal.
An elven wholesaler sits on a plank waiting for a count and is polite about it.

rooms.summary: Where the road from Caelum meets the reed-line. A wooden boardwalk runs out into the fen; from here you can see the reed-cutters at work in the middle distance.
ambientPool: A reed-cutter waves from far out in the fen, holding up a bundle.
An elven wholesaler shades their eyes against the late sun.
Three crates of pressed reed-fiber wait at the boardwalk's end for pickup.
A water-bird calls; the cutter pauses, then resumes.

exits.narrative: Inland to the rendering yard where the wax is made.

rooms.summary: Open yard with three iron kettles set on stone. Reed-fiber boils slowly under low fires. The yard-master tastes the steam, not the wax — they will tell you the season's quality by smell alone.
ambientPool: A kettle is skimmed with a long-handled paddle; pure wax dribbles into a clay form.
The yard-master nods at a kettle and says one quiet word — the reed has come from the right pool.
An elven wholesaler signs a slip; six crates of wax will be on a barge by morning.
A young apprentice tends the fires with the patient attention of someone learning a single craft for life.
